170: // HasGlobalAccess checks user access with globally assigned permissions only
171: func HasGlobalAccess(ac AccessControl, authnService authn.Service, c *contextmodel.ReqContext) func(evaluator Evaluator) bool {
172: 	return func(evaluator Evaluator) bool {
173: 		var targetOrgID int64 = GlobalOrgID
174: 		orgUser, err := authnService.ResolveIdentity(c.Req.Context(), targetOrgID, c.GetID())
175: 		if err != nil {
176: 			// This will be an common error for entities that can't authenticate in global scope
177: 			c.Logger.Debug("Failed to authenticate user in global scope", "error", err)
178: 			return false
179: 		}
180: 
181: 		hasAccess, err := ac.Evaluate(c.Req.Context(), orgUser, evaluator)
182: 		if err != nil {
183: 			c.Logger.Error("Error from access control system", "error", err)
184: 			return false
185: 		}
186: 
187: 		// guard against nil map
188: 		if c.Permissions == nil {
189: 			c.Permissions = make(map[int64]map[string][]string)
190: 		}
191: 		// set on user so we don't fetch global permissions every time this is called
192: 		c.Permissions[orgUser.GetOrgID()] = orgUser.GetPermissions()
193: 
194: 		return hasAccess
195: 	}
196: }
197: 
198: func HasAccess(ac AccessControl, c *contextmodel.ReqContext) func(evaluator Evaluator) bool {
199: 	return func(evaluator Evaluator) bool {
200: 		hasAccess, err := ac.Evaluate(c.Req.Context(), c.SignedInUser, evaluator)
201: 		if err != nil {
202: 			c.Logger.Error("Error from access control system", "error", err)
203: 			return false
204: 		}
205: 
206: 		return hasAccess
207: 	}
208: }

220: func ReqHasRole(role org.RoleType) func(c *contextmodel.ReqContext) bool {

224: func BuildPermissionsMap(permissions []Permission) map[string]bool {
225: 	permissionsMap := make(map[string]bool)
226: 	for _, p := range permissions {
227: 		permissionsMap[p.Action] = true
228: 	}
229: 
230: 	return permissionsMap
231: }

233: // GroupScopesByAction will group scopes on action
234: //
235: // Deprecated: use GroupScopesByActionContext instead
236: func GroupScopesByAction(permissions []Permission) map[string][]string {
237: 	return GroupScopesByActionContext(context.Background(), permissions)
238: }
239: 
240: // GroupScopesByAction will group scopes on action
241: func GroupScopesByActionContext(ctx context.Context, permissions []Permission) map[string][]string {
242: 	_, span := tracer.Start(ctx, "accesscontrol.GroupScopesByActionContext", trace.WithAttributes(
243: 		attribute.Int("permissions_count", len(permissions)),
244: 	))
245: 	defer span.End()
246: 
247: 	m := make(map[string][]string)
248: 	for i := range permissions {
249: 		m[permissions[i].Action] = append(m[permissions[i].Action], permissions[i].Scope)
250: 	}
251: 	return m
252: }
253: 
254: // Reduce will reduce a list of permissions to its minimal form, grouping scopes by action
255: func Reduce(ps []Permission) map[string][]string {
256: 	reduced := make(map[string][]string)
257: 	scopesByAction := make(map[string]map[string]bool)
258: 	wildcardsByAction := make(map[string]map[string]bool)
259: 
260: 	// helpers
261: 	add := func(scopesByAction map[string]map[string]bool, action, scope string) {
262: 		if _, ok := scopesByAction[action]; !ok {
263: 			scopesByAction[action] = map[string]bool{scope: true}
264: 			return
265: 		}
266: 		scopesByAction[action][scope] = true
267: 	}
268: 	includes := func(wildcardsSet map[string]bool, scope string) bool {
269: 		for wildcard := range wildcardsSet {
270: 			if wildcard == "*" || strings.HasPrefix(scope, wildcard[:len(wildcard)-1]) {
271: 				return true
272: 			}
273: 		}
274: 		return false
275: 	}
276: 
277: 	// Sort permissions (scopeless, wildcard, specific)
278: 	for i := range ps {
279: 		if ps[i].Scope == "" {
280: 			if _, ok := reduced[ps[i].Action]; !ok {
281: 				reduced[ps[i].Action] = nil
282: 			}
283: 			continue
284: 		}
285: 		if isWildcard(ps[i].Scope) {
286: 			add(wildcardsByAction, ps[i].Action, ps[i].Scope)
287: 			continue
288: 		}
289: 		add(scopesByAction, ps[i].Action, ps[i].Scope)
290: 	}
291: 
292: 	// Reduce wildcards
293: 	for action, wildcards := range wildcardsByAction {
294: 		for wildcard := range wildcards {
295: 			if wildcard == "*" {
296: 				reduced[action] = []string{wildcard}
297: 				break
298: 			}
299: 			if includes(wildcards, wildcard[:len(wildcard)-2]) {
300: 				continue
301: 			}
302: 			reduced[action] = append(reduced[action], wildcard)
303: 		}
304: 	}
305: 
306: 	// Reduce specific
307: 	for action, scopes := range scopesByAction {
308: 		for scope := range scopes {
309: 			if includes(wildcardsByAction[action], scope) {
310: 				continue
311: 			}
312: 			reduced[action] = append(reduced[action], scope)
313: 		}
314: 	}
315: 
316: 	return reduced
317: }
318: 
319: func ValidateScope(scope string) bool {
320: 	prefix, last := scope[:len(scope)-1], scope[len(scope)-1]
321: 	// verify that last char is either ':' or '/' if last character of scope is '*'
322: 	if len(prefix) > 0 && last == '*' {
323: 		lastChar := prefix[len(prefix)-1]
324: 		if lastChar != ':' && lastChar != '/' {
325: 			return false
326: 		}
327: 	}
328: 	return !strings.ContainsAny(prefix, "*?")
329: }
330: 
331: func ManagedUserRoleName(userID int64) string {
332: 	return fmt.Sprintf("managed:users:%d:permissions", userID)
333: }
334: 
335: func ManagedTeamRoleName(teamID int64) string {
336: 	return fmt.Sprintf("managed:teams:%d:permissions", teamID)
337: }
338: 
339: func ManagedBuiltInRoleName(builtInRole string) string {
340: 	return fmt.Sprintf("managed:builtins:%s:permissions", strings.ToLower(builtInRole))
341: }
342: 
343: // GetOrgRoles returns legacy org roles for a user
344: func GetOrgRoles(user identity.Requester) []string {
345: 	roles := []string{string(user.GetOrgRole())}
346: 
347: 	if user.GetIsGrafanaAdmin() {
348: 		if user.GetOrgID() == GlobalOrgID {
349: 			// A server admin is the admin of the global organization
350: 			return []string{RoleGrafanaAdmin, string(org.RoleAdmin)}
351: 		}
352: 		roles = append(roles, RoleGrafanaAdmin)
353: 	}
354: 
355: 	return roles
356: }
357: 
358: // PermissionsForActions generate Permissions for all actions provided scoped to provided scope.
359: func PermissionsForActions(actions []string, scope string) []Permission {
360: 	permissions := make([]Permission, len(actions))
361: 
362: 	for i, action := range actions {
363: 		permissions[i] = Permission{
364: 			Action: action,
365: 			Scope:  scope,
366: 		}
367: 	}
368: 
369: 	return permissions
370: }
371: 
372: func BackgroundUser(name string, orgID int64, role org.RoleType, permissions []Permission) identity.Requester {
373: 	return &user.SignedInUser{
374: 		OrgID:   orgID,
375: 		OrgRole: role,
376: 		Login:   "grafana_" + name,
377: 		Permissions: map[int64]map[string][]string{
378: 			orgID: GroupScopesByAction(permissions),
379: 		},
380: 	}
381: }

siuPermissions[accesscontrol.ActionUsersPermissionsRead]\n575: \t\tif !ok {\n576: \t\t\treturn func(_ int64) bool { return false }\n577: \t\t}\n578: \n579: \t\tids := map[int64]bool{}\n580: \t\tfor i 170: func resolveDashboardScope(ctx context.Context, orgID int64, dashboard *Dashboard, folderSvc folder.Service) ([]string, error) {
171: 	ctx, span := tracer.Start(ctx, "dashboards.resolveDashboardScope")
172: 	span.End()
173: 
174: 	var folderUID string

177: 	} else {
178: 		folderUID = dashboard.FolderUID
179: 	}
180: 
181: 	result, err := GetInheritedScopes(ctx, orgID, folderUID, folderSvc)
182: 	if err != nil {

189: 	},
190: 		result...,
191: 	)

196: func GetInheritedScopes(ctx context.Context, orgID int64, folderUID string, folderSvc folder.Service) ([]string, error) {
197: 	ctx, span := tracer.Start(ctx, "dashboards.GetInheritedScopes")
198: 	span.End()
199: 
200: 	if folderUID == ac.GeneralFolderUID {
201: 		return nil, nil
202: 	}
203: 	ancestors, err := folderSvc.GetParents(ctx, folder.GetParentsQuery{
204: 		UID:   folderUID,
205: 		OrgID: orgID,
206: 	})
207: 
208: 	if err != nil {
209: 		if errors.Is(err, folder.ErrFolderNotFound) {
210: 			return nil, err
211: 		}
212: 		return nil, ac.ErrInternal.Errorf("could not retrieve folder parents: %w", err)
213: 	}
214: 
215: 	result := make([]string, 0, len(ancestors))
216: 	for _, ff := range ancestors {
217: 		result = append(result, ScopeFoldersProvider.GetResourceScopeUID(ff.UID))
218: 	}
219: 
220: 	return result, nil
221: }

35: 	sql := `

38: 		  FROM dashboard

46: 		return err

Requesting a condensation of the conversation history.

Summary: USER_CONTEXT: Audit Grafana codebase to find all Go source files defining and enforcing the three-tier permission model (Viewer/Editor/Admin): role-to-permission mapping, permission guard middleware, and folder/dashboard permission inheritance.

CURRENT_STATE: Investigating repo `github.com/sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec`. Key files identified so far:

**Role Definition/Mapping:**
- `pkg/apimachinery/identity/role_type.go` — defines `RoleType` string type, constants `RoleNone/RoleViewer/RoleEditor/RoleAdmin`, `rolePrecedence` map, `IsValid()`, `Includes()`, `Children()`, `Parents()`, `UnmarshalText()`
- `pkg/services/org/model.go` — re-exports `RoleType` and role constants from identity package; defines `OrgUser`, `RoleType = identity.RoleType`
- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/roles.go` — defines fixed role DTOs, `BuildBasicRoleDefinitions()` (maps Admin/Editor/Viewer/None to basic role structs), `ValidateBuiltInRoles()`, `ValidateFixedRole()`

**Permission Guard Middleware:**
- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/middleware.go` — `Middleware()` function (permission guard), `authorize()`, `deny()`, `ReqHasRole()`, `UseOrgFromRequestData()`, `UseGlobalOrgFromRequestData()`

**Folder/Dashboard Permission Inheritance:**
- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/dashboard.go` — `DashboardPermissionsService`, `DashboardViewActions/EditActions/AdminActions`, `registerDashboardRoles()`, `ProvideDashboardPermissions()` with `InheritedScopesSolver` for folder inheritance
- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/folder.go` — `FolderPermissionsService`, `FolderViewActions/EditActions/AdminActions`, `registerFolderRoles()`, `ProvideFolderPermissions()` with `InheritedScopesSolver`
- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/service.go` — `Service`, `New()`, `ActionSetSvc`, `StoreActionSet()`, `ExpandActionSets()`, `GetActionSetName()`

**Supporting Files:**
- `pkg/api/accesscontrol.go` — `declareFixedRoles()` for HTTPServer
- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/accesscontrol.go` — `PermissionsService` interface
- `pkg/services/authn/identity.go` — `Identity.HasRole()`, `GetOrgRole()`
- `pkg/services/user/identity.go` — `SignedInUser.HasRole()`, `GetOrgRole()`
- `pkg/services/apiserver/auth/authorizer/role.go` — role-based authorizer
- `pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/accesscontrol/admin_only.go` — migration removing viewer/editor permissions

PENDING: 
- Read `pkg/services/accesscontrol/acimpl/` for core AC implementation
- Check `pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/store.go` for permission store
- Check `pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/middleware.go`
- Check `pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/models.go`
- Check `pkg/services/accesscontrol/models.go`
- Look for `RequireGrafanaAdmin`, `ReqEditorRole`, `ReqOrgAdmin` middleware
- Check `pkg/middleware/` for role-based guards
- Write final `answer.json`

30: func Middleware(ac AccessControl) func(Evaluator) web.Handler {
31: 	return func(evaluator Evaluator) web.Handler {
32: 		return func(c *contextmodel.ReqContext) {
33: 			ctx, span := tracer.Start(c.Req.Context(), "accesscontrol.Middleware")
34: 			defer span.End()
35: 			c.Req = c.Req.WithContext(ctx)
36: 
37: 			if c.AllowAnonymous {
38: 				forceLogin, _ := strconv.ParseBool(c.Req.URL.Query().Get("forceLogin")) // ignoring error, assuming false for non-true values is ok.
39: 				orgID, err := strconv.ParseInt(c.Req.URL.Query().Get("orgId"), 10, 64)
40: 				if err == nil && orgID > 0 && orgID != c.GetOrgID() {
41: 					forceLogin = true
42: 				}
43: 
44: 				if !c.IsSignedIn && forceLogin {
45: 					unauthorized(c)
46: 					return
47: 				}
48: 			}
49: 
50: 			if c.LookupTokenErr != nil {
51: 				var revokedErr *usertoken.TokenRevokedError
52: 				if errors.As(c.LookupTokenErr, &revokedErr) {
53: 					tokenRevoked(c, revokedErr)
54: 					return
55: 				}
56: 
57: 				unauthorized(c)
58: 				return
59: 			}
60: 
61: 			authorize(c, ac, c.SignedInUser, evaluator)
62: 		}
63: 	}
64: }

66: func authorize(c *contextmodel.ReqContext, ac AccessControl, user identity.Requester, evaluator Evaluator) {
67: 	ctx, span := tracer.Start(c.Req.Context(), "accesscontrol.authorize")
68: 	defer span.End()
69: 	c.Req = c.Req.WithContext(ctx)
70: 
71: 	injected, err := evaluator.MutateScopes(ctx, scopeInjector(scopeParams{
72: 		OrgID:     user.GetOrgID(),
73: 		URLParams: web.Params(c.Req),
74: 	}))
75: 	if err != nil {
76: 		c.JsonApiErr(http.StatusInternalServerError, "Internal server error", err)
77: 		return
78: 	}
79: 
80: 	hasAccess, err := ac.Evaluate(ctx, user, injected)
81: 	if !hasAccess || err != nil {
82: 		deny(c, injected, err)
83: 		return
84: 	}
85: }

87: func deny(c *contextmodel.ReqContext, evaluator Evaluator, err error) {
88: 	id := newID()
89: 	if err != nil {
90: 		c.Logger.Error("Error from access control system", "error", err, "accessErrorID", id)
91: 		// Return 404s for dashboard not found errors, our plugins rely on being able to distinguish between access denied and not found.
92: 		var dashboardErr dashboardaccess.DashboardErr
93: 		if ok := errors.As(err, &dashboardErr); ok {
94: 			if c.IsApiRequest() && dashboardErr.StatusCode == http.StatusNotFound {
95: 				c.JSON(http.StatusNotFound, map[string]string{
96: 					"title":   "Not found", // the component needs to pick this up
97: 					"message": dashboardErr.Error(),
98: 				})
99: 				return
100: 			}
101: 		}
102: 	} else {
103: 		c.Logger.Info(
104: 			"Access denied",
105: 			"id", c.GetID(),
106: 			"accessErrorID", id,
107: 			"permissions", evaluator.GoString(),
108: 		)
109: 	}
110: 
111: 	if !c.IsApiRequest() {
112: 		// TODO(emil): I'd like to show a message after this redirect, not sure how that can be done?
113: 		if !c.UseSessionStorageRedirect {
114: 			writeRedirectCookie(c)
115: 			c.Redirect(setting.AppSubUrl + "/")
116: 			return
117: 		}
118: 
119: 		c.Redirect(setting.AppSubUrl + "/" + getRedirectToQueryParam(c))
120: 		return
121: 	}
122: 
123: 	message := ""
124: 	if evaluator != nil {
125: 		message = evaluator.String()
126: 	}
127: 
128: 	// If the user triggers an error in the access control system, we
129: 	// don't want the user to be aware of that, so the user gets the
130: 	// same information from the system regardless of if it's an
131: 	// internal server error or access denied.
132: 	c.JSON(http.StatusForbidden, map[string]string{
133: 		"title":         "Access denied", // the component needs to pick this up
134: 		"message":       fmt.Sprintf("You'll need additional permissions to perform this action. Permissions needed: %s", message),
135: 		"accessErrorId": id,
136: 	})
137: }

139: func unauthorized(c *contextmodel.ReqContext) {
140: 	if c.IsApiRequest() {
141: 		c.WriteErrOrFallback(http.StatusUnauthorized, http.StatusText(http.StatusUnauthorized), c.LookupTokenErr)
142: 		return
143: 	}
144: 
145: 	if !c.UseSessionStorageRedirect {
146: 		writeRedirectCookie(c)
147: 	}
148: 
149: 	if errors.Is(c.LookupTokenErr, authn.ErrTokenNeedsRotation) {
150: 		if !c.UseSessionStorageRedirect {
151: 			c.Redirect(setting.AppSubUrl + "/user/auth-tokens/rotate")
152: 			return
153: 		}
154: 		c.Redirect(setting.AppSubUrl + "/user/auth-tokens/rotate" + getRedirectToQueryParam(c))
155: 		return
156: 	}
157: 
158: 	if !c.UseSessionStorageRedirect {
159: 		c.Redirect(setting.AppSubUrl + "/login")
160: 		return
161: 	}
162: 
163: 	c.Redirect(setting.AppSubUrl + "/login" + getRedirectToQueryParam(c))
164: }

166: func tokenRevoked(c *contextmodel.ReqContext, err *usertoken.TokenRevokedError) {
167: 	if c.IsApiRequest() {
168: 		c.JSON(http.StatusUnauthorized, map[string]any{
169: 			"message": "Token revoked",
170: 			"error": map[string]any{
171: 				"id":                    "ERR_TOKEN_REVOKED",
172: 				"maxConcurrentSessions": err.MaxConcurrentSessions,
173: 			},
174: 		})
175: 		return
176: 	}
177: 
178: 	if !c.UseSessionStorageRedirect {
179: 		writeRedirectCookie(c)
180: 		c.Redirect(setting.AppSubUrl + "/login")
181: 		return
182: 	}
183: 
184: 	c.Redirect(setting.AppSubUrl + "/login" + getRedirectToQueryParam(c))
185: }

187: func writeRedirectCookie(c *contextmodel.ReqContext) {
188: 	redirectTo := c.Req.RequestURI
189: 	if setting.AppSubUrl != "" && !strings.HasPrefix(redirectTo, setting.AppSubUrl) {
190: 		redirectTo = setting.AppSubUrl + c.Req.RequestURI
191: 	}
192: 
193: 	// remove any forceLogin=true params
194: 	redirectTo = removeForceLoginParams(redirectTo)
195: 
196: 	cookies.WriteCookie(c.Resp, "redirect_to", url.QueryEscape(redirectTo), 0, nil)
197: }

199: func getRedirectToQueryParam(c *contextmodel.ReqContext) string {
200: 	redirectTo := c.Req.RequestURI
201: 	if setting.AppSubUrl != "" && strings.HasPrefix(redirectTo, setting.AppSubUrl) {
202: 		redirectTo = strings.TrimPrefix(redirectTo, setting.AppSubUrl)
203: 	}
204: 
205: 	if redirectTo == "/" {
206: 		return ""
207: 	}
208: 
209: 	// remove any forceLogin=true params
210: 	redirectTo = removeForceLoginParams(redirectTo)
211: 	return "?redirectTo=" + url.QueryEscape(redirectTo)
212: }

214: var forceLoginParamsRegexp = regexp.MustCompile(`&?forceLogin=true`)

216: func removeForceLoginParams(str string) string {
217: 	return forceLoginParamsRegexp.ReplaceAllString(str, "")
218: }

220: func newID() string {
221: 	// Less ambiguity than alphanumerical.
222: 	numerical := []byte("0123456789")
223: 	id, err := util.GetRandomString(10, numerical...)
224: 	if err != nil {
225: 		// this should not happen, but if it does, a timestamp is as
226: 		// useful as anything.
227: 		id = fmt.Sprintf("%d", time.Now().UnixNano())
228: 	}
229: 	return "ACE" + id
230: }

234: func AuthorizeInOrgMiddleware(ac AccessControl, authnService authn.Service) func(OrgIDGetter, Evaluator) web.Handler {
235: 	return func(getTargetOrg OrgIDGetter, evaluator Evaluator) web.Handler {
236: 		return func(c *contextmodel.ReqContext) {
237: 			ctx, span := tracer.Start(c.Req.Context(), "accesscontrol.AuthorizeInOrgMiddleware")
238: 			defer span.End()
239: 			c.Req = c.Req.WithContext(ctx)
240: 
241: 			targetOrgID, err := getTargetOrg(c)
242: 			if err != nil {
243: 				if errors.Is(err, ErrInvalidRequestBody) || errors.Is(err, ErrInvalidRequest) {
244: 					c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{
245: 						"message": err.Error(),
246: 						"traceID": tracing.TraceIDFromContext(c.Req.Context(), false),
247: 					})
248: 					return
249: 				}
250: 				deny(c, nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to get target org: %w", err))
251: 				return
252: 			}
253: 
254: 			var orgUser identity.Requester = c.SignedInUser
255: 			if targetOrgID != c.GetOrgID() {
256: 				orgUser, err = authnService.ResolveIdentity(c.Req.Context(), targetOrgID, c.GetID())
257: 				if err == nil && orgUser.GetOrgID() == NoOrgID {
258: 					// User is not a member of the target org, so only their global permissions are relevant
259: 					orgUser, err = authnService.ResolveIdentity(c.Req.Context(), GlobalOrgID, c.GetID())
260: 				}
261: 				if err != nil {
262: 					deny(c, nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to authenticate user in target org: %w", err))
263: 					return
264: 				}
265: 			}
266: 			authorize(c, ac, orgUser, evaluator)
267: 
268: 			// guard against nil map
269: 			if c.Permissions == nil {
270: 				c.Permissions = make(map[int64]map[string][]string)
271: 			}
272: 			c.Permissions[orgUser.GetOrgID()] = orgUser.GetPermissions()
273: 		}
274: 	}
275: }

I am thinking...: Now I have a comprehensive picture of all the relevant files. Let me organize the findings for the final answer.json.

The three-tier permission model (Viewer/Editor/Admin) in Grafana is implemented across these key files:

## 1. Role Definition/Mapping

### `pkg/apimachinery/identity/role_type.go`
- Defines `RoleType` string type
- Constants: `RoleNone="None"`, `RoleViewer="Viewer"`, `RoleEditor="Editor"`, `RoleAdmin="Admin"`
- `rolePrecedence` map: None=10, Viewer=20, Editor=30, Admin=40
- Methods: `IsValid()`, `Includes()` (precedence-based), `Children()`, `Parents()`, `UnmarshalText()`

### `pkg/services/org/model.go`
- Re-exports `RoleType` and role constants from identity package
- `type RoleType = identity.RoleType`

### `pkg/services/accesscontrol/roles.go`
- Defines role name prefixes: `BasicRolePrefix="basic:"`, `FixedRolePrefix="fixed:"`, `ManagedRolePrefix="managed:"`
- `BuildBasicRoleDefinitions()` — creates the in-memory map of Admin/Editor/Viewer/None/GrafanaAdmin → `RoleDTO` structs (empty permissions, populated later via `DeclareFixedRoles`)
- `ValidateBuiltInRoles()`, `ValidateFixedRole()`
- Fixed role DTOs (ldapReaderRole, orgUsersWriterRole, etc.)

### `pkg/services/accesscontrol/models.go`
- `Permission` struct: `{Action, Scope}`
- `ResourcePermission` struct: `{Actions, Scope, UserID, TeamID, BuiltInRole, IsInherited, ...}`
- `RoleRegistration` struct: `{Role RoleDTO, Grants []string, Exclude []string}`
- `BuiltInRolesWithParents()` — expands a role to include all parent roles using `org.RoleType.Parents()`
- `GetOrgRoles()` — returns the org roles for a user (including GrafanaAdmin if applicable)
- `ManagedBuiltInRoleName()` — generates names like `"managed:builtins:viewer:permissions"`

### `pkg/services/accesscontrol/accesscontrol.go`
- `AccessControl` interface: `Evaluate()`, `RegisterScopeAttributeResolver()`, `WithoutResolvers()`
- `Service` interface: `GetUserPermissions()`, `DeclareFixedRoles()`, `GetStaticRoles()`, etc.
- `Store` interface: `GetUserPermissions()`, `GetBasicRolesPermissions()`, `GetTeamsPermissions()`, etc.
- `ReqGrafanaAdmin`, `ReqSignedIn`, `ReqHasRole()` — request-level role check helpers
- `HasAccess()`, `HasGlobalAccess()` — evaluator helpers

## 2. Permission Guard Middleware

### `pkg/services/accesscontrol/middleware.go`
- `Middleware(ac AccessControl) func(Evaluator) web.Handler` — the primary RBAC guard; checks token, then calls `authorize()`
- `authorize()` — injects URL params into scope templates, calls `ac.Evaluate()`, calls `deny()` on failure
- `deny()` — returns HTTP 403 with "Access denied" message
- `AuthorizeInOrgMiddleware()` — cross-org authorization middleware
- `UseOrgFromRequestData()`, `UseGlobalOrgFromRequestData()`, `UseGlobalOrg()`, `UseGlobalOrSingleOrg()` — org ID getters for middleware
- `scopeInjector()` — fills in URL param templates in scopes (e.g., `settings:{{.URLParams.:id}}`)

### `pkg/middleware/auth.go`
- `Auth(options *AuthOptions) web.Handler` — checks `ReqSignedIn`, `ReqGrafanaAdmin` flags
- `RoleAuth(roles ...org.RoleType) web.Handler` — legacy role-based guard (exact match on `c.OrgRole`)
- `RoleAppPluginAuth()` — plugin page auth (RBAC action or legacy role)
- `CanAdminPlugins()` — plugin admin guard

### `pkg/services/accesscontrol/acimpl/accesscontrol.go`
- `AccessControl` struct implementing `accesscontrol.AccessControl`
- `Evaluate()` / `evaluate()` — resolves user permissions, calls `evaluator.Evaluate(permissions)`, then tries scope resolution via `MutateScopes()`

### `pkg/services/accesscontrol/evaluator.go`
- `Evaluator` interface: `Evaluate()`, `EvaluateCustom()`, `MutateScopes()`
- `EvalPermission(action, scopes...)` — creates a `permissionEvaluator`
- `permissionEvaluator.Evaluate()` — checks if user has the action, then matches scopes (with wildcard support)
- `EvalAll()`, `EvalAny()` — composite evaluators

### `pkg/services/apiserver/auth/authorizer/role.go`
- `roleAuthorizer.Authorize()` — Kubernetes-style authorizer for the API server
- Admin → allow all verbs; Editor → allow get/list/watch/create/update/patch/delete/put/post; Viewer/None → allow get/list/watch only

## 3. Folder/Dashboard Permission Inheritance

### `pkg/services/dashboards/accesscontrol.go`
- Action constants: `ActionFoldersRead/Write/Delete/Create`, `ActionDashboardsRead/Write/Delete/Create`, `ActionFoldersPermissionsRead/Write`, `ActionDashboardsPermissionsRead/Write`
- `GetInheritedScopes()` — calls `folderSvc.GetParents()` to walk the folder tree and return parent folder UID scopes
- `resolveDashboardScope()` — resolves a dashboard to `[dashboards:uid:<uid>, folders:uid:<folderUID>, ...parent folder scopes]`
- `NewFolderIDScopeResolver()`, `NewFolderUIDScopeResolver()` — `ScopeAttributeResolver` implementations that expand folder scopes to include parent scopes
- `NewDashboardIDScopeResolver()`, `NewDashboardUIDScopeResolver()` — expand dashboard scopes to include folder and parent folder scopes

### `pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/folder.go`
- `FolderViewActions` = `[folders:read, alert.rules:read, library.panels:read, alert.silences:read]`
- `FolderEditActions` = View + `[folders:write, folders:delete, dashboards:create, alert.rules:create/update/delete, alert.silences:create/write, library.panels:create/write/delete]`
- `FolderAdminActions` = Edit + `[folders.permissions:read, folders.permissions:write]`
- `registerFolderRoles()` — declares fixed roles `fixed:folders:viewer/editor/admin` granted to Viewer/Editor/Admin org roles
- `ProvideFolderPermissions()` — wires up `resourcepermissions.Service` with `InheritedScopesSolver` calling `GetInheritedScopes()`

### `pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/dashboard.go`
- `DashboardViewActions`, `DashboardEditActions`, `DashboardAdminActions`
- `registerDashboardRoles()` — declares fixed roles `fixed:dashboards:viewer/editor/admin`
- `ProvideDashboardPermissions()` — wires up `resourcepermissions.Service` with `InheritedScopesSolver` calling `GetInheritedScopes()` for the dashboard's parent folder

### `pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/service.go`
- `Service` struct — manages resource-level permissions (user/team/built-in role assignments)
- `New()` — constructor taking `Options` including `InheritedScopesSolver`
- `GetPermissions()` — fetches permissions for a resource, including inherited ones
- `SetBuiltInRolePermission()` — sets permission for a built-in role (Viewer/Editor/Admin) on a resource

### `pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/store.go`
- `flatResourcePermission.IsManaged()` — checks if permission is a managed (resource-specific) permission
- `flatResourcePermission.IsInherited()` — returns true for managed permissions from parent resources (scope doesn't match directly)
- SQL queries for getting/setting resource permissions

### `pkg/services/accesscontrol/acimpl/service.go`
- `Service` struct — the core RBAC service
- `getUserPermissions()` — merges basic role permissions (from RAM) + DB permissions (managed/external)
- `getBasicRolePermissions()` — fetches permissions for a basic role (from RAM + DB managed roles)
- `getCachedUserPermissions()` — cached version combining basic role + team + user direct permissions
- `registerRolesLocked()` — adds fixed role permissions to the basic role's permission set
- `DeclareFixedRoles()` — registers fixed roles and their grants to basic roles
- `DeclarePluginRoles()` — registers plugin roles

## 4. Supporting Files

### `pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/accesscontrol/dashboard_permissions.go`
- `dashboardPermissionTranslation` — maps legacy PERMISSION_VIEW/EDIT/ADMIN to RBAC actions
- `folderPermissionTranslation` — same for folders
- `dashboardPermissionsMigrator` — migrates legacy ACL to RBAC managed permissions
- `managedFolderAlertActionsMigrator`, `managedDashboardAnnotationActionsMigrator`, `managedFolderAlertingSilencesActionsMigrator` — add new actions to existing managed permissions

### `pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/accesscontrol/admin_only.go`
- `adminOnlyMigrator` — removes viewer/editor managed permissions from dashboards/folders that have `has_acl=true` and empty ACL (admin-only access)

### `pkg/api/accesscontrol.go`
- `declareFixedRoles()` — declares all fixed roles for the HTTP server (org users, LDAP, server stats, etc.)

### `pkg/services/authn/identity.go`
- `Identity.HasRole()`, `GetOrgRole()` — role check methods on the authn identity

### `pkg/services/user/identity.go` (SignedInUser)
- `HasRole()`, `GetOrgRole()` — role check methods on SignedInUser

- Action constants: `ActionFoldersRead/Write/Delete/Create`, `ActionDashboardsRead/Write/Delete/Create`, `ActionFoldersPermissionsRead/Write`, `ActionDashboardsPermissionsRead/Write`
- `GetInheritedScopes()` — calls `folderSvc.GetParents()` to walk the folder tree and return parent folder UID scopes
- `resolveDashboardScope()` — resolves a dashboard to `[dashboards:uid:<uid>, folders:uid:<folderUID>, ...parent folder scopes]`
- `NewFolderIDScopeResolver()`, `NewFolderUIDScopeResolver()` — `ScopeAttributeResolver` implementations that expand folder scopes to include parent scopes
- `NewDashboardIDScopeResolver()`, `NewDashboardUIDScopeResolver()` — expand dashboard scopes to include folder and parent folder scopes

### `pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/folder.go`
- `FolderViewActions` = `[folders:read, alert.rules:read, library.panels:read, alert.silences:read]`
- `FolderEditActions` = View + `[folders:write, folders:delete, dashboards:create, alert.rules:create/update/delete, alert.silences:create/write, library.panels:create/write/delete]`
- `FolderAdminActions` = Edit + `[folders.permissions:read, folders.permissions:write]`
- `registerFolderRoles()` — declares fixed roles `fixed:folders:viewer/editor/admin` granted to Viewer/Editor/Admin org roles
- `ProvideFolderPermissions()` — wires up `resourcepermissions.Service` with `InheritedScopesSolver` calling `GetInheritedScopes()`

### `pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/dashboard.go`
- `DashboardViewActions`, `DashboardEditActions`, `DashboardAdminActions`
- `registerDashboardRoles()` — declares fixed roles `fixed:dashboards:viewer/editor/admin`
- `ProvideDashboardPermissions()` — wires up `resourcepermissions.Service` with `InheritedScopesSolver` calling `GetInheritedScopes()` for the dashboard's parent folder

### `pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/service.go`
- `Service` struct — manages resource-level permissions (user/team/built-in role assignments)
- `New()` — constructor taking `Options` including `InheritedScopesSolver`
- `GetPermissions()` — fetches permissions for a resource, including inherited ones
- `SetBuiltInRolePermission()` — sets permission for a built-in role (Viewer/Editor/Admin) on a resource

### `pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/store.go`
- `flatResourcePermission.IsManaged()` — checks if permission is a managed (resource-specific) permission
- `flatResourcePermission.IsInherited()` — returns true for managed permissions from parent resources (scope doesn't match directly)
- SQL queries for getting/setting resource permissions

### `pkg/services/accesscontrol/acimpl/service.go`
- `Service` struct — the core RBAC service
- `getUserPermissions()` — merges basic role permissions (from RAM) + DB permissions (managed/external)
- `getBasicRolePermissions()` — fetches permissions for a basic role (from RAM + DB managed roles)
- `getCachedUserPermissions()` — cached version combining basic role + team + user direct permissions
- `registerRolesLocked()` — adds fixed role permissions to the basic role's permission set
- `DeclareFixedRoles()` — registers fixed roles and their grants to basic roles
- `DeclarePluginRoles()` — registers plugin roles

## 4. Supporting Files

### `pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/accesscontrol/dashboard_permissions.go`
- `dashboardPermissionTranslation` — maps legacy PERMISSION_VIEW/EDIT/ADMIN to RBAC actions
- `folderPermissionTranslation` — same for folders
- `dashboardPermissionsMigrator` — migrates legacy ACL to RBAC managed permissions
- `managedFolderAlertActionsMigrator`, `managedDashboardAnnotationActionsMigrator`, `managedFolderAlertingSilencesActionsMigrator` — add new actions to existing managed permissions

### `pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/accesscontrol/admin_only.go`
- `adminOnlyMigrator` — removes viewer/editor managed permissions from dashboards/folders that have `has_acl=true` and empty ACL (admin-only access)

### `pkg/api/accesscontrol.go`
- `declareFixedRoles()` — declares all fixed roles for the HTTP server (org users, LDAP, server stats, etc.)

### `pkg/services/authn/identity.go`
- `Identity.HasRole()`, `GetOrgRole()` — role check methods on the authn identity

### `pkg/services/user/identity.go` (SignedInUser)
- `HasRole()`, `GetOrgRole()` — role check methods on SignedInUser

        "Service struct {store, ac, options Options, actionSetService ActionSetService, ...}",
        "Options struct {Resource, ResourceAttribute string, InheritedScopesSolver func(ctx, orgID, resourceID) ([]string, error), PermissionsToActions map[string][]string, Assignments Assignments, ResourceValidator func(...) error}",
        "New(cfg, options, ...) (*Service, error) — constructor; registers fixed reader/writer roles for the resource",
        "GetPermissions(ctx, user, resourceID) ([]ResourcePermission, error) — fetches direct + inherited permissions, marks inherited ones via IsInherited",
        "SetUserPermission(), SetTeamPermission(), SetBuiltInRolePermission() — upsert permission for a principal on a resource",
        "SetPermissions() — batch set permissions for multiple principals",
        "MapActions(permission ResourcePermission) string — maps action set back to friendly name (View/Edit/Admin)",
        "ActionSetSvc / StoreActionSet() / ExpandActionSets() — action set expansion for efficient DB storage"
      ]
    },
    {
      "file": "pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/store.go",
      "purpose": "SQL store for resource permissions with inheritance detection",
      "key_symbols": [
        "store struct {cfg, sql db.DB, features}",
        "flatResourcePermission struct — raw DB row: RoleName, Action, Scope, UserId, TeamId, BuiltInRole, IsServiceAccount",
        "flatResourcePermission.IsManaged(scope) bool — true if role name has 'managed:' prefix AND scope matches exactly (direct permission)",
        "flatResourcePermission.IsInherited(scope) bool — true if role name has 'managed:' prefix AND scope does NOT match (permission inherited from a parent resource)",
        "SetUserResourcePermission(), SetTeamResourcePermission(), SetBuiltInRoleResourcePermission() — upsert managed role permissions in DB",
        "GetResourcePermissions() — SQL query joining role+permission+user/team tables, filtered by resource scope + inherited scopes from InheritedScopesSolver",
        "DeleteResourcePermissions() — removes all permissions for a resource scope"
      ]
    },
    {
      "file": "pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/middleware.go",
      "purpose": "Placeholder middleware for resource permissions (nop implementation)",
      "key_symbols": [
        "nopMiddleware(c *contextmodel.ReqContext) — no-op middleware used as default hook"
      ]
    },
    {
      "file": "pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/accesscontrol/dashboard_permissions.go",
      "purpose": "Migration: translates legacy dashboard ACL (View/Edit/Admin) to RBAC managed permissions",
      "key_symbols": [
        "dashboardPermissionTranslation map[PermissionType][]string — PERMISSION_VIEW→[dashboards:read], PERMISSION_EDIT→[dashboards:read/write/delete], PERMISSION_ADMIN→[dashboards:read/write/create/delete/permissions:read/write]",
        "folderPermissionTranslation map[PermissionType][]string — same but also includes folders:read/write/delete/permissions:read/write",
        "dashboardPermissionsMigrator.Exec() — reads dashboard_acl table, creates managed:builtins:editor/viewer:permissions roles with translated RBAC actions",
        "dashboardUidPermissionMigrator — migrates id-based scopes (dashboards:id:N) to uid-based scopes (dashboards:uid:X)",
        "managedFolderCreateAction — removes folders:create from scoped managed permissions",
        "managedFolderAlertActionsMigrator — adds alert.rules:* actions to existing managed folder permissions",
        "managedDashboardAnnotationActionsMigrator — adds annotations:* actions to managed dashboard permissions",
        "managedFolderAlertingSilencesActionsMigrator — adds alert.silences:* actions to managed folder permissions"
      ]
    },
    {
      "file": "pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/accesscontrol/admin_only.go",
      "purpose": "Migration: removes viewer/editor managed permissions from admin-only dashboards/folders",
      "key_symbols": [
        "AddAdminOnlyMigration(mg) — registers 'admin only folder/dashboard permission' migration",
        "adminOnlyMigrator.Exec() — finds dashboards/folders with has_acl=true and empty dashboard_acl (admin-only access pattern), then DELETEs permissions from managed:builtins:editor:permissions and managed:builtins:viewer:permissions roles for those scopes"
      ]
    },
    {
      "file": "pkg/api/accesscontrol.go",
      "purpose": "Declares all fixed roles for the HTTP API server",
      "key_symbols": [
        "declareFixedRoles(service accesscontrol.Service) error — calls service.DeclareFixedRoles() for all fixed roles used by the HTTP server: org users reader/writer, LDAP reader/writer, server stats reader, settings reader/writer, etc., each with Grants specifying which org roles (Viewer/Editor/Admin/Grafana Admin) receive them"
      ]
    }
  ]
}

The `answer.json` is valid and complete. Here is a full summary of the findings:

---

## Grafana Three-Tier Permission Model — Complete Audit

### 1. Role Definition & Mapping (7 files)

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `pkg/apimachinery/identity/role_type.go` | **Canonical type definition.** Defines `RoleType` string, constants `RoleNone/Viewer/Editor/Admin`, `rolePrecedence` map (None=10, Viewer=20, Editor=30, Admin=40), and methods `IsValid()`, `Includes()` (precedence-based containment), `Children()`, `Parents()`, `UnmarshalText()` |
| `pkg/services/org/model.go` | Re-exports `RoleType` as a type alias from the identity package |
| `pkg/services/accesscontrol/roles.go` | Defines role name prefixes (`basic:`, `fixed:`, `managed:`), `BuildBasicRoleDefinitions()` (creates empty Admin/Editor/Viewer/None/GrafanaAdmin `RoleDTO` stubs), `ValidateBuiltInRoles()`, `ValidateFixedRole()` |
| `pkg/services/accesscontrol/models.go` | Core data models: `Permission{Action,Scope}`, `RoleRegistration{Role,Grants}`, `ResourcePermission{IsInherited}`, `BuiltInRolesWithParents()`, `GetOrgRoles()`, `ManagedBuiltInRoleName()` |
| `pkg/services/accesscontrol/accesscontrol.go` | `AccessControl`/`Service`/`Store` interfaces; `ReqGrafanaAdmin`, `ReqSignedIn`, `ReqHasRole()`, `HasAccess()`, `HasGlobalAccess()` |
| `pkg/services/accesscontrol/acimpl/service.go` | **Core RBAC service.** `getUserPermissions()` merges RAM basic-role permissions + DB managed permissions; `registerRolesLocked()` appends fixed role permissions to basic role stubs; `DeclareFixedRoles()` registers role→grant mappings |
| `pkg/services/accesscontrol/acimpl/accesscontrol.go` | `AccessControl.evaluate()` — fetches user permissions, calls `evaluator.Evaluate()`, then tries scope resolution via `MutateScopes()` with registered resolvers |

### 2. Permission Guard Middleware (6 files)

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `pkg/services/accesscontrol/middleware.go` | **Primary RBAC guard.** `Middleware(ac)` → `authorize()` → `ac.Evaluate()` → `deny()` (HTTP 403). Also `AuthorizeInOrgMiddleware()` for cross-org checks, `scopeInjector()` for URL-param template filling, and org-ID getter helpers |
| `pkg/middleware/auth.go` | Legacy guards: `Auth(AuthOptions)` checks `ReqSignedIn`/`ReqGrafanaAdmin`; `RoleAuth(roles...)` does exact org-role match; `RoleAppPluginAuth()` bridges RBAC and legacy role checks for plugins |
| `pkg/services/accesscontrol/evaluator.go` | `Evaluator` interface; `EvalPermission(action, scopes...)` with wildcard scope matching; `EvalAll()` (AND) and `EvalAny()` (OR) composites |
| `pkg/services/apiserver/auth/authorizer/role.go` | Kubernetes-style `roleAuthorizer.Authorize()`: Admin→all verbs allowed; Editor→read+write verbs; Viewer/None→read-only verbs |
| `pkg/services/authn/identity.go` | `Identity.HasRole()`, `GetOrgRole()` |
| `pkg/services/user/identity.go` | `SignedInUser.HasRole()` (uses `Includes()` for hierarchy), `GetOrgRole()` |

### 3. Folder/Dashboard Permission Inheritance (9 files)

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `pkg/services/dashboards/accesscontrol.go` | **Inheritance engine.** `GetInheritedScopes()` calls `folderSvc.GetParents()` to walk the ancestor chain and return parent `folders:uid:<X>` scopes. Four `ScopeAttributeResolver` implementations (`NewFolderIDScopeResolver`, `NewFolderUIDScopeResolver`, `NewDashboardIDScopeResolver`, `NewDashboardUIDScopeResolver`) expand any folder/dashboard scope to include all ancestor folder scopes |
| `pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/folder.go` | `FolderViewActions`, `FolderEditActions`, `FolderAdminActions` action sets; `registerFolderRoles()` declares `fixed:folders:viewer/editor/admin`; `ProvideFolderPermissions()` wires `InheritedScopesSolver` → `GetInheritedScopes()` |
| `pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/dashboard.go` | `DashboardViewActions/EditActions/AdminActions`; `registerDashboardRoles()`; `ProvideDashboardPermissions()` with `InheritedScopesSolver` for parent folder lookup |
| `pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/service.go` | Generic resource permission service: `GetPermissions()` fetches direct+inherited; `SetBuiltInRolePermission()` assigns View/Edit/Admin to a resource for a built-in role |
| `pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/store.go` | `flatResourcePermission.IsManaged(scope)` / `IsInherited(scope)` — distinguishes direct vs. inherited managed permissions by comparing the stored scope against the requested scope |
| `pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/middleware.go` | `nopMiddleware` — placeholder hook |
| `pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/accesscontrol/dashboard_permissions.go` | Migrates legacy ACL `PERMISSION_VIEW/EDIT/ADMIN` → RBAC actions; adds alert/annotation/silence actions to existing managed permissions |
| `pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/accesscontrol/admin_only.go` | Removes viewer/editor managed permissions from dashboards/folders that have `has_acl=true` with empty ACL (admin-only pattern) |
| `pkg/api/accesscontrol.go` | `declareFixedRoles()` — registers all HTTP-server fixed roles with their Viewer/Editor/Admin grants |

| `pkg/services/accesscontrol/acimpl/accesscontrol.go` | `AccessControl.evaluate()`: fetches user permissions, calls `evaluator.Evaluate()`, then tries scope resolution via `MutateScopes()` with registered resolvers |

---

### Section 2 — Permission Guard Middleware (6 files)

| File | What it does |
|---|---|
| `pkg/services/accesscontrol/middleware.go` | Primary RBAC guard: `Middleware(ac)` → `authorize()` → `ac.Evaluate()` → `deny()` (HTTP 403); `AuthorizeInOrgMiddleware()` for cross-org; `scopeInjector()` for URL-param template filling |
| `pkg/middleware/auth.go` | `Auth(AuthOptions)` checks `ReqSignedIn`/`ReqGrafanaAdmin`; `RoleAuth(roles...)` exact org-role match; `RoleAppPluginAuth()` bridges RBAC and legacy role checks |
| `pkg/services/accesscontrol/evaluator.go` | `Evaluator` interface; `EvalPermission(action, scopes...)` with wildcard scope matching; `EvalAll()` (AND) and `EvalAny()` (OR) composites |
| `pkg/services/apiserver/auth/authorizer/role.go` | K8s-style `roleAuthorizer`: Admin→all verbs; Editor→read+write verbs; Viewer/None→read-only verbs |
| `pkg/services/authn/identity.go` | `Identity.HasRole()`, `GetOrgRole()` |
| `pkg/services/user/identity.go` | `SignedInUser.HasRole()` (uses `Includes()` for hierarchy), `GetOrgRole()` |

### Section 3 — Folder/Dashboard Permission Inheritance (10 files)

| File | What it does |

| `pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/service.go` | Generic resource permission service: `GetPermissions()` fetches direct+inherited; `SetBuiltInRolePermission()` assigns View/Edit/Admin to a resource |
| `pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/store.go` | `IsManaged(scope)` / `IsInherited(scope)` distinguish direct vs. inherited managed permissions by comparing stored scope against requested scope |
| `pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/models.go` | `GetResourcePermissionsQuery.InheritedScopes []string` carries parent folder UID scopes from `InheritedScopesSolver` into the SQL store |

| `pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/accesscontrol/dashboard_permissions.go` | Migrates legacy ACL `PERMISSION_VIEW/EDIT/ADMIN` → RBAC actions; adds alert/annotation/silence actions to existing managed permissions |
| `pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/accesscontrol/admin_only.go` | Removes viewer/editor managed permissions from admin-only dashboards/folders (`has_acl=true`, empty ACL) |
| `pkg/api/accesscontrol.go` | `declareFixedRoles()` registers all HTTP-server fixed roles with their Viewer/Editor/Admin grants |
